title: Can ‘Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 3’ Salvage Marvel’s No Good, Very Bad Year?
format: Sean Fennessey and Marvel historian Joanna Robinson on a studio in a wobble (the box-office slide, the Victoria Alonso exit, the Jonathan Majors problem) and why James Gunn's swan song, on his way to DC, is the best thing Marvel has put out in years.

films.title: Ant-Man and the Wasp: Quantumania
films.note: The roughly $475M international take that read as a floodgates moment for the brand. Fennessey admits he liked it more than most.

films.title: Iron Man
films.note: The launch. Van Lathan's argument, cited here: Tony Stark's voice set the quippy house style every later hero inherited.

films.title: Avengers: Infinity War
films.note: Gunn wrote the Guardians' dialogue in it, over Marcus and McFeely's script, an exception Marvel makes only for him.

films.title: Avengers: Endgame
films.note: The peak. So many contractual character deaths that Robinson reads Vol. 3's refusal to kill anyone as a reaction to it.

films.title: Spider-Man: Far From Home
films.note: The last MCU film before a roughly two-year gap. Robinson brackets the Sony co-productions as something other than the MCU proper.

films.title: Black Widow
films.note: Smaller box office coming out of the theater-closure period; the Scarlett Johansson lawsuit that dented the friendly-corporation image.

films.title: Shang-Chi and the Legend of the Ten Rings
films.note: Named among directors hired without much prior interest in this kind of storytelling, and the seams that shows.

films.title: Eternals
films.note: Robinson's pick for one of the worst of the post-pandemic era; the kitchen-table problem when the cast has never spoken to each other before.

films.title: Doctor Strange in the Multiverse of Madness
films.note: Fennessey is fonder of it than most, for the Sam Raimi touches.

films.title: Thor: Love and Thunder
films.note: Shot in the volume; held up as a cautionary tale of the cosmic-era VFX crunch.

films.title: Black Panther: Wakanda Forever
films.note: A good movie weighed down by franchise homework: Ironheart, an untitled Wakanda series, Julia Louis-Dreyfus.

films.title: Black Panther
films.note: Coogler cast as another inspired choice, a mainstream thinker who loved comics, before the franchising load arrived.

films.title: Captain Marvel
films.note: A movie Fennessey likes, minus the 'No Doubt' needle drop he calls one of the most offensive in cinema: 90s grave-robbing.

films.title: The Marvels
films.note: Delayed and in reshoots, opening in November, the same week Robinson's Marvel book drops.

films.title: Magazine Dreams
films.note: Jonathan Majors's acclaimed Sundance performance, once floated as an Oscar contender; its December release now in doubt after his arrest.

films.title: Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 3
films.note: Both hosts' favorite post-Endgame Marvel movie by a distance. Real sets over the volume, Rocket's origin as the heart, the MCU's first F-bomb, and a hard PG-13 with body horror Fennessey wouldn't show a seven-year-old. Also its most purposeful business decision: a post-credits card announcing Star-Lord will return.

films.title: Guardians of the Galaxy
films.note: The creative risk that proved Marvel could sell something weird. Per Robinson, most Marvel staff name it, not Iron Man, the studio's most important film.

films.title: Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 2
films.note: Robinson rewatched it before taping; it introduced Mantis, whose emotional load grows in Vol. 3.

referenced.group: James Gunn, off the Marvel clock
Best trilogy capper (a Trial by Content poll)
films: The Lord of the Rings: The Return of the King
Indiana Jones and the Last Crusade

Disney+ specials: The Guardians of the Galaxy Holiday Special

Shang-Chi and the Legend of the Ten Ring.title: Shang-Chi and the Legend of the Ten Rings

Doctor Strange in the Multiverse of Madn.title: Doctor Strange in the Multiverse of Madness
